title: The Plaint Human
content:
Season of snows, and season of flowers,
            Seasons of loss and gain! - 
        Since grief and joy must alike be ours,
            Why do we still complain?

        Ever our failing, from sun to sun,
            O my intolerent brother: - 
        We want just a little too little of one,
            And much too much of the other.
meaning:
This evocative piece by James Whitcomb Riley, titled "The Plaint Human", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
